lessons.title: List Comprehensions
lessons.concept_intro: A shortcut for creating lists from other lists in one line. Pythonic and powerful.
lessons.analogy: Like a factory production line: raw material goes in one end, finished product comes out the other, all in one step.

lessons.title: String Methods Deep Dive
lessons.concept_intro: Strings have dozens of methods: split, join, strip, replace, startswith, endswith, and more.
lessons.analogy: A Swiss Army knife for text — different tools for every text job.

lessons.title: Modules & Imports
lessons.concept_intro: Modules let you use code that others wrote. Import a module and use its functions.
lessons.analogy: Like borrowing a tool from a neighbor — someone already built it, just use it.

lessons.title: Installing Packages
lessons.concept_intro: pip installs packages from the internet. Virtual environments keep projects separate.
lessons.analogy: Like an app store for Python — thousands of free tools, one command to install.

lessons.title: Working with APIs
lessons.concept_intro: APIs let your program talk to other services over the internet — weather, news, translation, anything.
lessons.analogy: Like ordering food delivery: you send a request (order), wait, and get data (food) back.

project.title: Weather App
project.description: Fetch real weather data from an API and display it nicely.
